title: Non-Surgical Body Contouring in Malta: What Your Options Actually Are (And How to Choose)
excerpt: Daniela has been looking at the same spot on her abdomen all summer. She's not overweight. She runs three times a week, eats well by most people's standards, and yet there is an area that has sat there for years: soft, resistant, utterly indifferent to her efforts. She has googled her way into confusion. Fat freezing, fat dissolving, EMSculpt, VelaShape. Every clinic page reads like a different argument for the same miracle. She does not know what any of these things actually do to her body....
body_text:
Daniela has been looking at the same spot on her abdomen all summer.

She's not overweight. She runs three times a week, eats well by most people's standards, and yet there is an area that has sat there for years: soft, resistant, utterly indifferent to her efforts. She has googled her way into confusion. Fat freezing, fat dissolving, EMSculpt, VelaShape. Every clinic page reads like a different argument for the same miracle. She does not know what any of these things actually do to her body. She does not know which one, if any, is right for her.

She is not alone. This is where most people are when they first reach out to us.

The end of summer is a natural moment for this kind of clarity. You have spent weeks in swimwear. You know exactly which areas respond to exercise and which ones simply do not. That is not a failure of effort. Subcutaneous fat in certain areas is metabolically stubborn and resists calorie restriction disproportionately. This is recognised biology, not an excuse.

Non-surgical body contouring in Malta refers to clinically studied, non-invasive procedures that target specific tissue — without surgery, downtime, or general anaesthesia. This post breaks down what each main option actually does at tissue level, who it suits, and how to decide what is right for your goals.

[Image: EMSculpt NEO]

What Is Non-Surgical Body Contouring?
Non-surgical body contouring is the umbrella term for procedures that reduce, reshape, or redefine areas of the body without incisions, surgery, or general anaesthesia. At Carisma Slimming, the main categories available are cryolipolysis (fat freezing), injectable lipolysis (fat dissolving injections), HIFEM muscle stimulation (EMSculpt), and anti-cellulite treatment Malta via device-based treatment (VelaShape).

Before going any further, one distinction matters enormously. Body contouring is not a weight loss treatment. It is a body shaping treatment. These are not interchangeable. Body contouring targets specific tissue in defined areas. Weight loss refers to reducing overall body fat mass through a caloric deficit or metabolic intervention. A person whose primary need is significant fat reduction would not be well-served by body contouring alone. A person at or near a stable weight, with localised areas that have not responded to exercise, may be an excellent candidate.

This is not a disclaimer. It is the most important piece of information on this page. Understanding it is the difference between the right treatment and a frustrating, expensive experience.

The NHS notes that cosmetic body contouring procedures are elective treatments and are not funded publicly, which underlines that a suitability assessment by a qualified practitioner is essential before beginning any course of treatment.


Fat Freezing (Cryolipolysis) in Malta
We have covered fat freezing Malta in detail in our dedicated guide. The brief version: cryolipolysis uses controlled cooling to trigger apoptosis (programmed fat cell death) in targeted fat deposits. The body's lymphatic system then clears the affected cells over subsequent weeks. The evidence base is well-established. Manstein D et al. (2008) published the foundational research on selective cryolysis in Lasers in Surgery and Medicine, and multiple randomised controlled trials have followed since.

Fat freezing works best for localised, pinchable fat deposits that have not responded to diet and exercise: the lower abdomen, flanks, inner thighs, bra area. It is not suitable if there is no pinchable fat in the target area, if you have certain cold-related conditions, or during pregnancy.

Results may vary for each individual. Fat freezing does not reduce overall body weight. It reduces the volume of fat in specific, treated areas.


Fat Dissolving Injections in Malta
Fat dissolving Malta injections use injectable lipolysis to disrupt fat cell membranes in small, defined areas. The solution breaks down the fat cell wall, releasing fatty acids that the lymphatic system then processes and clears from the body.

This approach works particularly well for smaller, more defined areas: the double chin, bra fat, inner thighs, and lower abdomen. Multiple sessions are typically required, and post-treatment bruising and swelling are a normal, expected part of the process — not a warning sign.

The best-evidenced formulation for injectable lipolysis is sodium deoxycholate (ATX-101), which holds both FDA and EMA approval specifically for submental (under-chin) fat reduction. For other body areas and formulations, evidence varies by area and formulation. A clinical consultation is important precisely because the right approach depends on the specific area and individual anatomy.

Like fat freezing, fat dissolving injections are not weight loss interventions. They address localised deposits, not overall body fat percentage.


EMSculpt in Malta
EMSculpt Malta uses high-intensity focused electromagnetic (HIFEM) technology to induce supramaximal muscle contractions in targeted muscle groups — contractions beyond what voluntary exercise can produce. Research by Kinney BM and colleagues, published in the Journal of Drugs in Dermatology, has demonstrated both the safety and efficacy of HIFEM technology for muscle building and toning.

The primary outcome of EMSculpt is muscle building and definition, not fat reduction in isolation. There is some secondary fat reduction in the treatment area, and EMSculpt NEO adds radiofrequency for simultaneous fat layer reduction alongside the muscle stimulation effect. Best results are typically seen on the abdomen and buttocks.

This makes EMSculpt a distinct category from the fat reduction treatments above. It is body sculpting in the truest sense: building definition where muscle density has reduced or where exercise alone has not produced the results a person is looking for.

Contraindications include metallic implants, pacemakers, and pregnancy. These are non-negotiable exclusions that will be confirmed at consultation. For a full breakdown of EMSculpt NEO specifically, we have covered that in a dedicated post.


VelaShape and Anti-Cellulite Treatment
VelaShape uses a combination of infrared light, bipolar radiofrequency energy, vacuum, and mechanical massage delivered simultaneously to address cellulite and skin texture concerns. It targets the fibrous septae — the connective tissue bands responsible for the dimpled appearance of cellulite — and stimulates lymphatic circulation in the treatment area.

This is a different category from fat reduction or muscle building. VelaShape's primary focus is skin texture and connective tissue, not fat volume or muscle mass. A full course of sessions is typically recommended for meaningful results.

VelaShape suits women with mild to moderate cellulite who want to improve the texture and smoothness of the skin's surface. It is not a fat loss treatment and should not be selected on that basis. For broader body reshaping alongside skin texture improvement, this is one option that can complement others within a personalised programme.


How to Choose the Right Body Contouring Treatment for You
This is the section most people want to jump to directly. The honest answer: there is no universal right answer, and anyone who tells you otherwise without assessing you individually is not giving you honest guidance.

That said, a practical framework helps.

Start with your primary goal. Is your concern localised fat that has not responded to exercise? Fat freezing or fat dissolving injections may be the right starting point. Is it muscle definition or toning in a specific area? EMSculpt is the relevant category. Is it skin texture, dimpling, or cellulite appearance? VelaShape and anti-cellulite body shaping treatment are the appropriate direction.

Consider the area. Fat freezing requires a pinchable fat deposit. Fat dissolving works best on smaller, defined areas. EMSculpt works specifically on muscle groups (abdomen, buttocks). These distinctions determine suitability before anything else.

Be honest about whether weight loss is the actual goal. Body contouring is not a substitute for a medically supervised slimming programme. If the primary goal is overall fat reduction, that is the right starting point, not body contouring treatments. The two can complement each other as part of a broader plan, but they are not the same thing. Using them interchangeably wastes time and money.

Book a consultation. At our Malta clinic, we assess your specific concerns, explain what is and is not realistic for your anatomy, and we are honest if a particular treatment is not the right fit — even if that means recommending a different approach entirely. The best treatment is the one that matches your individual goal, area, and starting point.

Results may vary for each individual. What produces clear results for one person in one area may not produce the same outcomes in a different area or for a different person. A personalised approach is not a nice-to-have. It is the only approach that actually works.


Frequently Asked Questions — Body Contouring Malta
Is non-surgical body contouring permanent?
Fat cells reduced through cryolipolysis or fat dissolving injections do not regenerate. In that sense, the reduction in those specific cells is lasting. Remaining fat cells in the area can still expand with significant weight gain, so results are best maintained alongside a healthy lifestyle. Muscle tone achieved through EMSculpt requires ongoing exercise to sustain, just as gym-built muscle does.

How many sessions does body contouring take?
This depends entirely on the treatment and the individual. Fat freezing is often effective over one to three sessions per area. Fat dissolving injections typically require multiple sessions spaced several weeks apart. EMSculpt courses are generally four sessions over two weeks. VelaShape anti-cellulite treatment is typically recommended as a course of six or more sessions. Your practitioner will advise on the right number of sessions for your specific concern at consultation.

Is body contouring painful?
Most people find non-surgical body contouring treatments tolerable, and some describe them as comfortable. Fat freezing involves an intense cold sensation that typically numbs within minutes. Fat dissolving injections can produce some discomfort and bruising during and after the session. EMSculpt produces a powerful muscular contraction that is unusual but not typically described as painful. VelaShape feels similar to a warm, firm massage. Your practitioner will walk you through what to expect before any treatment begins.

Can I lose weight with body contouring?
Body contouring is not a weight loss treatment. This is one of the most important distinctions to understand before booking any procedure. Body contouring targets specific tissue in defined areas. It does not reduce overall body fat mass, does not replace the effects of dietary change, and should not be selected as a weight loss solution. If your primary goal is weight reduction, the right starting point is a medical weight loss programme that addresses the metabolic and lifestyle factors involved. Body contouring can complement a weight loss journey by targeting residual localised areas once weight has been stabilised — but it is not a substitute, and it will not produce the outcomes a genuine weight loss programme would.

What is the difference between body contouring and liposuction?
Liposuction is a surgical procedure performed under general or local anaesthesia that mechanically removes fat cells from the body. It produces more substantial changes in a single procedure and carries the risks associated with surgery and anaesthesia, including a recovery period. Non-surgical body contouring treatments achieve more modest, gradual results without surgery, anaesthesia, or downtime. For those who want to avoid surgery, non-surgical body contouring in Malta offers a clinically supported alternative for defined, localised concerns. The right choice depends on the degree of change sought, tolerance for recovery, and medical suitability.


The Right Treatment Is the One That Matches You
Not every body contouring option is right for every person. And not every concern even needs a body contouring treatment.

At Carisma Slimming in Malta, the consultation is where the real work begins. We look at the specific area, the goal, the person's overall health picture, and we give honest guidance. Sometimes that means a single treatment. Sometimes a combination. Sometimes it means starting somewhere entirely different.

Not sure which body contouring treatment in Malta is right for your goals? That is exactly what a free slimming consultation is for. We will look at your concerns, explain what is realistic, and build a plan around what will actually help — even if that means a combination of approaches, or starting somewhere different entirely.

Book your free slimming consultation today.
